records.ety: [OE. belongen (akin to D. belangen to concern, G. belangen to attain to, to concern); pref. be- + longen to desire. See Long, v. i.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Belonged (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Belonging.]

defs: To be the property of; as, Jamaica belongs to Great Britain.
To be a part of, or connected with; to be appendant or related; to owe allegiance or service.
quotes: A desert place belonging to . . . Bethsaids.
The mighty men which belonged to David.

defs: To be the concern or proper business or function of; to appertain to.
To be suitable for; to be due to.
quotes: Strong meat belongeth to them that are of full age.

defs: To be native to, or an inhabitant of; esp. to have a legal residence, settlement, or inhabitancy, whether by birth or operation of law, so as to be entitled to maintenance by the parish or town.
quotes: Bastards also are settled in the parishes to which the mothers belong.
More evils belong us than happen to us.
